content:
One of the most frequent roadblocks when performing an RMAN Duplicate is encountering file name conflicts, particularly when the destination directory structure already contains data from previous attempts.
The Issue While running the duplication process, RMAN fails with the following error stack:
RMAN-05541: no resource release ID during duplicate. RMAN-05001: name … is used by another file. This happens because RMAN detects that the datafiles it is trying to create already exist on the target server or are registered in a way that causes a naming collision.
The Solution: NOFILENAMECHECK The most straightforward way to resolve this (provided you are certain that the files at the destination can be overwritten) is to add the NOFILENAMECHECK clause at the end of your duplication command.
Implementation Example: RUN { ALLOCATE AUXILIARY CHANNEL aux1 DEVICE TYPE DISK; DUPLICATE TARGET DATABASE TO "DB_DEST" FROM ACTIVE DATABASE SPFILE PARAMETER_VALUE_CONVERT 'DB_SRC','DB_DEST' SET DB_FILE_NAME_CONVERT '/u01/oradata/DB_SRC/','/u02/oradata/DB_DEST/' NOFILENAMECHECK; -- Tells RMAN to skip the filename existence check }
summary:
One of the most frequent roadblocks when performing an RMAN Duplicate is encountering file name conflicts, particularly when the destination directory structure already contains data from previous attempts.

The Issue

While running the duplication process, RMAN fails with the following error stack:

  • RMAN-05541: no resource release ID during duplicate.
  • RMAN-05001: name … is used by another file.

This happens because RMAN detects that the datafiles it is trying to create already exist on the target server or are registered in a way that causes a naming collision.
title: How to Fix RMAN-05541 and RMAN-05001 During Database Duplicate

RMAN-05541: no archived logs found in target database. Problem: You are getting RMAN-05541 error when duplicating database from a consistent (cold) RMAN backup.
RMAN-00571: =========================================================== RMAN-00569: =============== ERROR MESSAGE STACK FOLLOWS =============== RMAN-00571: =========================================================== RMAN-03002: failure of Duplicate Db command at 04/16/2021 12:57:12 RMAN-05501: aborting duplication of target database RMAN-05541: no archived logs found in target database **Solution:** The error should be resolved by using NOREDO clause for your duplicate command. ```sql DUPLICATE DATABASE TO 'TEST02' BACKUP LOCATION '/mnt/export/oracle/orabackup/full/' NOREDO; ### RMAN-05501: aborting duplication of target database (Auxiliary file name /.../.../oracle/.../oradata/perfstat01.dbf conflicts with a file used by the target database). **Problem:** You are getting RMAN-05001 error when duplicating database from a consistent (cold) RMAN backup. ```text RMAN-00571: =========================================================== RMAN-00569: =============== ERROR MESSAGE STACK FOLLOWS =============== RMAN-00571: =========================================================== RMAN-03002: failure of Duplicate Db command at 04/16/2021 13:12:33 RMAN-05501: aborting duplication of target database RMAN-05001: auxiliary file name /mnt/export/oracle/oradata/perfstat01.dbf conflicts with a file used by the target database **Solution:** The error should be resolved by using NOFILENAMECHECK clause for your duplicate command. ```sql DUPLICATE DATABASE TO 'TEST02' BACKUP LOCATION '/mnt/export/oracle/orabackup/full/' NOFILENAMECHECK; 💡 Tip: Both can be used in the same DUPLICATE command in case we are duplicating non-OMF database from a cold RMAN backup as follows: ```sql DUPLICATE DATABASE TO 'TEST02' BACKUP LOCATION '/mnt/export/oracle/orabackup/full/' NOREDO NOFILENAMECHECK;
summary:
RMAN-05541: no archived logs found in target database.

Problem: You are getting RMAN-05541 error when duplicating database from a consistent (cold) RMAN backup.

RMAN-00571: ===========================================================
RMAN-00569: =============== ERROR MESSAGE STACK FOLLOWS ===============
RMAN-00571: ===========================================================
RMAN-03002: failure of Duplicate Db command at 04/16/2021 12:57:12
RMAN-05501: aborting duplication of target database
RMAN-05541: no archived logs found in target database

**Solution:** 
The error should be resolved by using NOREDO clause for your duplicate command.

```sql
DUPLICATE DATABASE TO 'TEST02' BACKUP LOCATION '/mnt/export/oracle/orabackup/full/' NOREDO;

### RMAN-05501: aborting duplication of target database (Auxiliary file name /.../.../oracle/.../oradata/perfstat01.dbf conflicts with a file used by the target database).

**Problem:** 
You are getting RMAN-05001 error when duplicating database from a consistent (cold) RMAN backup.
```text
RMAN-00571: ===========================================================
RMAN-00569: =============== ERROR MESSAGE STACK FOLLOWS ===============
RMAN-00571: ===========================================================
RMAN-03002: failure of Duplicate Db command at 04/16/2021 13:12:33
RMAN-05501: aborting duplication of target database
RMAN-05001: auxiliary file name /mnt/export/oracle/oradata/perfstat01.dbf conflicts with a file used by the target database

**Solution:** 
The error should be resolved by using NOFILENAMECHECK clause for your duplicate command.

```sql
DUPLICATE DATABASE TO 'TEST02' BACKUP LOCATION '/mnt/export/oracle/orabackup/full/' NOFILENAMECHECK;

💡 Tip: Both can be used in the same DUPLICATE command in case we are duplicating non-OMF database from a cold RMAN backup as follows:

```sql
DUPLICATE DATABASE TO 'TEST02' BACKUP LOCATION '/mnt/export/oracle/orabackup/full/' NOREDO NOFILENAMECHECK;
title: RMAN-05541 & RMAN-05001 when duplicating database from a consistent (cold) RMAN backup
